narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text directly criticizes the mayor's decision to travel to a climate summit, framing it as hypocritical and questioning the effectiveness and practical impact of his climate policy actions by highlighting the carbon cost of the trip and suggesting he should have focused on local problems instead.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.” A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended because of a potential landslide hazard.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges the competence and credibility of the mayor and his administration by pointing out the gap between their presentation on 'high-quality urban transportation infrastructure' and the reality of infrastructure failures and cancellations in Los Angeles.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Garcetti’s “Green New Deal” calls for shuttering natural gas plants; it has been opposed by public works unions, and lost the only serious electoral challenge it faced in a 2019 city council race.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement criticizes a specific climate policy initiative (the Green New Deal) by highlighting opposition from unions and an electoral loss, thereby challenging the political viability and support for the climate movement's proposals.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: That is one-fourth (economy) or one-half (premium) the global average per capita use of carbon per year. It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: By quantifying the trip's carbon footprint relative to an annual average and questioning the necessity of in-person attendance, the text minimizes the significance of the summit's purpose (combating climate change) and suggests the action is disproportionate or unnecessary.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote directly criticizes the mayor's action of flying to a climate summit, framing it as hypocritical and questioning the effectiveness and practical impact of his climate policy commitment by highlighting the carbon cost and opportunity cost of the trip.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Mayor Garcetti's competence and priorities by contrasting his international travel with domestic crises, challenging his integrity and effectiveness as a leader and the institution of his office.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement implicitly criticizes the climate movement (represented by the C40 summit) by questioning the necessity and sincerity of its actions, suggesting the event is performative rather than practically focused on reducing emissions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.” A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended because of a potential landslide hazard.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges the competence and credibility of Mayor Garcetti and his administration by pointing to failures in local transportation infrastructure, thereby undermining the authority's claims and actions presented at the climate summit.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
